title: <div>  •  Jake LaMotta (Raging Bull) – A boxer who destroys his personal life in
content:
• Jake LaMotta (Raging Bull) – A boxer who destroys his personal life in the name of his career.

Key Traits:
• Driven, obsessive, stubborn.
• Struggles with balance in life.
• Self-destructive tendencies.
• Isolated, emotionally detached.

2. The Mentor (The Harsh Teacher / The Tormentor)

This mentor isn’t nurturing—they push the protagonist to the breaking point because they believe greatness only emerges through suffering.

Examples:
• Fletcher (Whiplash) – Emotionally abusive, brilliant, and manipulative, yet fosters excellence.
• Takuan (Vagabond) – A philosophical guide who challenges Musashi’s brutal worldview.
• Mickey (Rocky) – Gruff but caring, pushes Rocky beyond his limits.

Key Traits:
• Brutal, unforgiving, emotionally withholding.
• Believes in pain as a form of teaching.
• Is the protagonist’s greatest ally and greatest enemy.

3. The Rival (The Reflection of Another Path)
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 11 (chunk 11/14)
